subject: english
learning_objectives: Define a pronoun and explain why pronouns are used
Identify and distinguish all major types of pronouns
Use each type of pronoun correctly in sentences

sections.heading: Definition
sections.content: Pronouns are words used to replace nouns in a sentence, mainly to avoid unnecessary repetition. Both nouns and pronouns perform the same grammatical functions and are used interchangeably. Examples: he, they, we.

sections.heading: Types of Pronouns
sections.content:
Personal Pronouns: I, we, they, us, he, she, it, them

Possessive Pronouns: yours, his, hers, theirs, its, ours

Demonstrative Pronouns: this, that, these, those

Interrogative Pronouns: who, which, whom, whose, what

Reflexive Pronouns: myself, themselves, yourselves, yourself, ourselves, oneself

Reciprocal Pronouns: each other, one another

Relative Pronouns: which, whom, whose, who, that

Indefinite Pronouns: someone, somebody, anything, anyone, everything, everyone, nobody, nothing
